display.purpose: Marquee total/valuation number and modal titles. Tops out ~1.5rem; no hero type in this dense product UI.

headline.purpose: Modal headings in Inter, tight tracking.

body.purpose: Descriptive paragraphs and panel help text in Inter, often at reduced starlight opacity; hints italic.

label.purpose: The signature voice: JetBrains Mono, uppercase, tracked. Eyebrows, status pills, badges, button text. Capped at ~4 words.

data.purpose: All numeric content in JetBrains Mono: dollar figures, quantities, rates, table cells. Currency right-aligned.

shadows.name: glass-hover-glow
shadows.purpose: Lift a glass panel gets on hover: faint blue bloom plus inner rim light.

shadows.name: panel-ambient
shadows.purpose: Resting dual-hue glow under floating instrument panels (.glass-panel-glow).

shadows.name: focus-glow
shadows.purpose: Cool-blue halo a frosted input emits on focus, replacing a hard ring.

shadows.name: orb-halo
shadows.purpose: Diffuse blue aura under the resting voice orb.

motion.name: ease-glass
motion.purpose: Default easing for state transitions on glass surfaces and inputs.

motion.name: ease-out-expo
motion.purpose: Snappy reveal easing: slide-in, panel-right, ring-expand.

motion.name: ease-barrel
motion.purpose: The orb's barrel-roll into theater mode (1.9s).

motion.name: duration-state
motion.purpose: Standard control state transition (hover, scale, brighten).

components.name: Primary Gradient CTA
components.description: Headline action (Subscribe, Dispatch Authorization). Cool-blue to violet gradient, void-black label.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display:inline-flex; align-items:center; gap:6px; background:linear-gradient(90deg,#6eb5ff,#a78bfa); color:#050810; font-family:'JetBrains Mono',ui-monospace,monospace; font-size:9px; font-weight:900; letter-spacing:0.15em; text-transform:uppercase; border:none; border-radius:9999px; padding:12px 20px; cursor:pointer; transition:transform 0.2s cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1), filter 0.2s; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { transform:scale(1.05); filter:brightness(1.08); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline:2px solid #6eb5ff; outline-offset:2px; } .ds-btn-primary:disabled { opacity:0.45; cursor:default; }

components.name: Publish (Charge-Up Secondary)
components.description: Dark navy gradient that fills with the brand gradient on hover and flips text to void-black.
components.html: <button class="ds-btn-publish"><svg width="14" height="14" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2.5" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><path d="M22 11.08V12a10 10 0 1 1-5.93-9.14"/><polyline points="22 4 12 14.01 9 11.01"/></svg>Publish & Send PDF</button>
components.css: .ds-btn-publish { display:inline-flex; align-items:center; gap:6px; background:linear-gradient(90deg,#20346a,#2e1d52); color:#e2e8f0; border:1px solid rgba(110,181,255,0.3); font-family:'JetBrains Mono',ui-monospace,monospace; font-size:9px; font-weight:900; letter-spacing:0.15em; text-transform:uppercase; border-radius:9999px; padding:12px 20px; cursor:pointer; box-shadow:0 10px 25px -5px rgba(110,181,255,0.05); transition:background 0.3s cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1), color 0.3s; } .ds-btn-publish:hover { background:linear-gradient(90deg,#6eb5ff,#a78bfa); color:#050810; } .ds-btn-publish:focus-visible { outline:2px solid #6eb5ff; outline-offset:2px; }

components.name: Frosted Conversational Input
components.description: Pill text field for 'describe your job'. Glows cool-blue on focus, no hard ring.
components.html: <input class="ds-input-frosted" type="text" placeholder="Describe materials, dimensions, or scope..." />
components.css: .ds-input-frosted { width:100%; background:rgba(255,255,255,0.03); border:1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.08); color:#e2e8f0; font-family:'JetBrains Mono',ui-monospace,monospace; font-size:11px; letter-spacing:0.02em; border-radius:9999px; padding:6px 16px; outline:none; transition:all 0.2s cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1); } .ds-input-frosted::placeholder { color:rgba(226,232,240,0.4); } .ds-input-frosted:focus { background:rgba(255,255,255,0.06); border-color:#6eb5ff; box-shadow:0 0 14px rgba(110,181,255,0.3); }

components.name: Price-Source Badge (AI)
components.description: Tint-on-tint pill marking where a price came from. ai=violet, override=blue, database=emerald.
components.css: .ds-badge-ai,.ds-badge-override,.ds-badge-db { font-family:'JetBrains Mono',ui-monospace,monospace; font-size:8px; font-weight:800; text-transform:uppercase; letter-spacing:0.1em; padding:1px 6px; border-radius:4px; } .ds-badge-ai { background:rgba(167,139,250,0.2); color:#a78bfa; } .ds-badge-override { background:rgba(110,181,255,0.2); color:#6eb5ff; } .ds-badge-db { background:rgba(52,211,153,0.2); color:#34d399; }

components.name: Glass Instrument Panel
components.description: Floating frosted panel over the starfield. Glow-as-depth, ghost border, no nesting.
components.css: .ds-glass-panel { background:rgba(10,15,30,0.65); backdrop-filter:blur(16px); -webkit-backdrop-filter:blur(16px); border:1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.12); border-radius:16px; padding:20px; color:#e2e8f0; box-shadow:0 8px 32px -4px rgba(110,181,255,0.08), 0 4px 16px -4px rgba(167,139,250,0.06); transition:border-color 0.3s, box-shadow 0.3s; } .ds-glass-panel:hover { border-color:rgba(110,181,255,0.25); box-shadow:0 8px 32px 0 rgba(110,181,255,0.08), 0 0 1px 1px rgba(110,181,255,0.15) inset; } .ds-panel-eyebrow { display:block; font-family:'JetBrains Mono',ui-monospace,monospace; font-size:8px; font-weight:900; letter-spacing:0.15em; text-transform:uppercase; color:#a78bfa; margin-bottom:12px; } .ds-panel-row { display:flex; justify-content:space-between; align-items:center; font-family:'JetBrains Mono',ui-monospace,monospace; font-size:11px; color:rgba(226,232,240,0.7); } .ds-panel-num { color:#6eb5ff; font-weight:800; }

components.name: Voice Orb (resting)
components.description: The console you talk to. Pulsing gradient orb with a cool-blue mic; the system's signature object.
components.html: <button class="ds-voice-orb" aria-label="Describe your job"><svg width="20" height="20" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="#6eb5ff" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><path d="M12 1a3 3 0 0 0-3 3v8a3 3 0 0 0 6 0V4a3 3 0 0 0-3-3z"/><path d="M19 10v2a7 7 0 0 1-14 0v-2"/><line x1="12" y1="19" x2="12" y2="23"/></svg></button>
components.css: .ds-voice-orb { height:56px; width:56px; border-radius:9999px; display:flex; align-items:center; justify-content:center; background:linear-gradient(45deg,#121829,#3a2254); border:1px solid #614582; cursor:pointer; box-shadow:0 20px 25px -5px rgba(110,181,255,0.10); animation:ds-orb-pulse 2s infinite ease-in-out; transition:all 0.3s; } .ds-voice-orb:hover { background:linear-gradient(45deg,#1b2641,#4e316d); } @keyframes ds-orb-pulse { 0% { transform:scale(1); box-shadow:0 0 0 0 rgba(110,181,255,0.4), 0 0 0 8px rgba(167,139,250,0.15), 0 0 0 20px rgba(110,181,255,0.1);} 50% { transform:scale(1.06); box-shadow:0 0 18px 6px rgba(110,181,255,0.5), 0 0 0 16px rgba(167,139,250,0.2), 0 0 0 35px rgba(110,181,255,0);} 100% { transform:scale(1); box-shadow:0 0 0 0 rgba(110,181,255,0.4), 0 0 0 0 rgba(167,139,250,0), 0 0 0 0 rgba(110,181,255,0);} } @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .ds-voice-orb { animation:none; } }

narrative.overview:
StudCast is the control deck of a one-person operation. The screen is a void-black hull; the work floats on glass instrument panels lit from within by cool-blue and violet readouts. A solo contractor sits at the helm of something that looks and feels far bigger than one person, which is the entire emotional job: a Lone Ranger should look established to their client and to themselves. The voice orb is the console you talk to; the ledger is the manifest; the 3D material yard is the viewport.

The system is confident and energetic, not quiet. Gradients run blue into violet, controls scale and brighten on contact, and the orb literally barrel-rolls across the bridge when it moves into theater mode. But spectacle never sits on top of the work: the motion and 3D orbit the ledger; the number the contractor is putting their name on stays still, legible, and trustworthy at the center.

This system explicitly rejects clunky legacy contractor software (dense gray QuickBooks/Buildertrend-era estimating tools that feel like data entry) and the generic SaaS dashboard (interchangeable indigo-on-white, card-grid, hero-metric Linear/Stripe clones). The cosmic-glass identity exists so this product is never mistaken for another seat-based B2B tool.
keyCharacteristics: Void-black space hull as the canvas; a real Milky Way photo, never a flat dark gray.
Glass instrument panels with backdrop blur, glowing rather than shadowed.
A two-voice accent system: cool-blue is trust (money, primary actions), soft-violet is intelligence (AI moments); together they form the brand gradient.
Monospace, tracked, uppercase micro-labels as the dominant typographic voice.
Expressive, confident motion (orb pulse, barrel-roll, listening rings) that orbits the work without blocking it.
The trusted number is always cool-blue, always still, always readable.

rules.name: The Two-Voice Rule
rules.body: Cool-blue means trust (money, totals, prices, primary actions, focus); soft-violet means intelligence (anything the AI did or proposes: extraction, change orders, the ai price source, sparkle flourishes). They combine into the brand gradient, but their meanings never blur. A violet total or a blue 'ai' badge is wrong; the color tells the contractor where the value came from.

rules.name: The Dark-On-Bright Rule
rules.body: Text on any cool-blue/violet gradient surface is void-black (#050810), never white. The gradient is the light source; the label is the silhouette on it. White-on-gradient is forbidden; it muddies the brand color and drops contrast.

rules.name: The One Blue Number Rule
rules.body: The figure the contractor trusts (grand total, line amounts, prices) is always cool-blue and never wrapped in motion. Money does not animate, gradient-fill, or glow-pulse. Its stillness is what makes it trustworthy.

rules.name: The No Flat Black Rule
rules.body: The background is the starfield photo over void-black, never a flat #050810 fill on a content screen. Depth comes from the nebula, the aurora pools, and the vignette. A flat dark-gray panel grid is the legacy-software tell and is prohibited.

rules.name: The Mono Label Rule
rules.body: Every label, status, badge, and number is JetBrains Mono, uppercase, tracked 0.1em-0.15em. Sans-serif is for sentences a human reads; mono is for everything the bridge reports.

rules.name: The Tracked-Caps Are Labels, Not Eyebrows Rule
rules.body: Uppercase tracked mono is a deliberate brand system here (the console voice), so the 'no eyebrow on every section' ban is satisfied by intent. But caps are capped at ~4 words. Never set a sentence or paragraph in tracked caps.

rules.name: The No Hard Shadow Rule
rules.body: Drop shadows that read as a panel lifting off a page are forbidden; that is the 2014-app / legacy-tool tell. If a surface needs to feel raised, it glows or it blurs the background. Audit test: if the shadow is dark, tight, and gray, it's wrong; the right one is large, soft, and blue.

dos: Do split the accents by meaning: cool-blue for trust (money, prices, primary actions), soft-violet for intelligence (AI moments). They share the gradient, not their meanings.
Do keep the trusted number cool-blue (#6eb5ff) and motionless. Money is still; stillness is trust.
Do put void-black text on every cool-blue/violet gradient surface, never white.
Do set every label, status, badge, and dollar figure in uppercase tracked JetBrains Mono; reserve Inter for sentences.
Do convey depth with glows, backdrop-blur, and the aurora/vignette starfield, never hard drop shadows.
Do use the three-color price-source code (violet ai / blue override / emerald database) so the contractor always sees where a price came from.
Do let the orb and 3D be expressive and energetic, but keep them orbiting the ledger, never on top of the number.
Do honor prefers-reduced-motion: the barrel-roll, orb pulse, listening rings, and bounce flashes all need a crossfade-or-instant fallback.
donts: Don't let it feel like clunky legacy contractor software: no dense gray spreadsheet sprawl, no nested menus, no data-entry screens.
Don't let it become a generic SaaS dashboard: no indigo-on-white, no repeated identical card grids, no hero-metric template, no Linear/Stripe-clone look.
Don't use a flat #050810 fill as a full-screen content background; the starfield and aurora carry the depth.
Don't nest glass panels inside glass panels; inner rows are flat void-black/40 tinted strips.
Don't cast hard, dark, tight drop shadows; if it's not large, soft, and tinted blue, it doesn't belong.
Don't set sentences or paragraphs in tracked uppercase; caps are for 4-word labels only.
Don't use alert-rose decoratively; it means destructive or error, nothing else.
